Koncept & Funktion is an underground Drum & Bass sample library produced by David & Nic Higham in the UK, that turns your computer into a super-powerful DnB production machine, with all the sounds you need to turn out unique professional quality dance tracks.

You'll feel like you've died and gone to heaven when you get your hands on this creative and highly useable Drum & Bass production library, which provides everything you'll need.

Find yourself creating superb unique tracks in no time at all and you'll be hard pressed to tear yourself away from your sampler!

Even if you're not interested in Drum & Bass, there's enough variety and flexibility to make this massive (over 1 GB) sample collection worth investigating for all kinds of music.

DnB producers will find plenty to keep them happy and they're likely to find themselves conjuring up a masterpiece before they know it.

title: Wizoo Platinum 24 Acoustic Drums For Akai S 1000
description:
This is a collection of one shot samples of an acoustic drum kit, multisampled at different velocity levels, assembled into kits. The original ISOs are a weird type of cd-rom that can't simply be read on a normal computer without the help of specialist sampler software like NI Kontakt or similar or the Akai hardware these were made for.

A rip made in 2007 that is presumed mostly complete is provided in the zip files, giving direct access to WAV versions of the samples.

title: Northstar Drumscapes Roland (Roland) ISO
description:
Northstar - Drumscapes Roland

Northstar Productions Drumscapes Vol 1 specs:

Year of release: 1992

Format:             Roland



Sample CD-ROM library in Roland S-770 and compatible format





Drumscapes Volume 1 is a collection of drum samples, including single hits and full grooves,
formatted on CD-ROM for the Roland S-770 and compatible samplers and sample players.

From the CD-ROM’s booklet:  “Drumscapes are direct to digital, live recordings of fully produced studio drum tracks.  
In addition, each song track includes every individual drum and cymbal hit for digital sampling.”

The recording quality is extremely good: the sounds are very punchy, full-bodied,
and the drum patterns are tight and perfectly looped.  
The single hits also are recorded very well, so it’s easy to create your own drum kits,
picking and choosing from the different volumes.

The vibe here is for the most part on the acoustic side of rock/pop,
with the occasional venture into hip-hop or Motown –  These samples are really good
and if you play any of the styles listed here  below, you will not be disappointed.

The styles included are: Rhythm & Blues Hip Hop/Rap Adult Contemporary/Light Jazz Ballad
Medium Rock Fast Heavy Rock Power Ballad Fast Shuffle Slow Shuffle Pop/Country Ballad
Fast Techno Rock Medium Techno Rock Uptempo Funk Slow Motown and as a bonus there’s also a full
set of percussion samples. In order to use these CD-ROM you will need a SCSI CD-ROM drive connected
to your S-series sampler or XV-5080. Keep in mind that certain sound data will need 32MB of memory,
supported only by the S-760. The S-770 and 750 have less.

title: Retro Sample CDS
description:
Here I will upload some of the old 90s early 00s sampling CDs in their respective format, or converted to any open domain sampler format (sf2, sfz - but mostly sfz) which can be played in various samplers or using Sforzando VST.  

I've made this decision because sometimes using an instance of e.g. Kontakt VST may be a little bit naive for just one 200kB patch. But that depends, so here you can mostly download original ISO or MDX images and then You can decide to what format you'll convert them.

One of the benefits of SFZ format is such that you can use either samples on their own or use given .sfz file in your sampler to unleash the true layering / grouping of each program patch.

title: Beckstrom - Korg Volca Modular drum kit samples
description:
# About

A collection of drum/percussion sounds created on the Korg Volca Modular synthesizer

It's a really fantastic synthesizer and I've barely scratched the surface! Before I bought one, I watched many demonstration/patching videos about it, and none of them tried synthesizing percussion. It definitely lends itself well to grungy atonal sequences, but it can also be coaxed into making percussion.

The main challenge in using it to create percussion sounds is the lack of a noise generator. I used a number of different approaches to get "noise," including feeding oscillators back into themselves and running the "woggle" module at audio rates. Other types of sounds like cowbell are much easier, for those I often just used some fine-tuning of the dual oscillator module. Because of its patchability and i/o, it would certainly be possible to use external oscillators and noise sources in conjunction with the onboard modules to get a more varied palette of sounds. Perhaps I'll do that and make a new sample set!

Recorded 2020-05-17
